ElementDescription: The NDAR Global Unique Identifier (GUID) for research subject
Subject ID how it's defined in lab/project
Date on which the interview/genetic test/sampling/imaging/biospecimen was completed. MM/DD/YYYY
Age in months at the time of the interview/test/sampling/imaging.
Notes: Age is rounded to chronological month. If the research participant is 15-days-old at time of interview, the appropriate value would be 0 months. If the participant is 16-days-old, the value would be 1 month.

ElementDescription: Sex of subject at birth
Notes: M = Male; F = Female; O=Other; NR = Not reported

ElementDescription: Daily assessment session number
Prompt/beep number (3 times a day for 7 days EMA period)
Since the last beep: Child: I felt GROUCHY or ANNOYED/Caregiver: My child felt GROUCHY or ANNOYED
Since the last beep: Child: I felt FRUSTRATED/Caregiver: My child felt FRUSTRATED
Since the last beep: Child: People/things were ANNOYING me/Caregiver: People/things were ANNOYING my child
Since the last beep: Child: I TALKED BACK or ARGUED with someone/Caregiver: My child TALKED BACK or ARGUED with someone
Since the last beep: Child: I LOST my TEMPER/Caregiver: My child LOST their TEMPER
Temper lost frequency - Rate only if response to ''temper'' is greater than 10.
During the worst time, how long did temper outburst last? Rate only if response to temper is greater than 10.
Rate only if response to temper is greater than 10. During the worst time, temper outburst behaviors (check all that apply) - Talked back, argued, called someone names
Rate only if response to temper is greater than 10. During the worst time, temper outburst behaviors (check all that apply) - Yelled, threatened
Rate only if response to temper is greater than 10. During the worst time, temper outburst behaviors (check all that apply) - Slammed, threw, kicked something
Rate only if response to temper is greater than 10. During the worst time, temper outburst behaviors (check all that apply) - Hit, pushed, kicked, bumped someone
Rate only if response to temper is greater than 10. During the worst time, temper outburst behaviors (check all that apply) - None of the above

Rate only if response to frust_events is Yes. Specify frustrating events (check all that apply) - Homework or school work
Rate only if response to frust_events is Yes. Specify frustrating events (check all that apply) - Failing to do something
Rate only if response to frust_events is Yes. Specify frustrating events (check all that apply) - Losing video games

Rate only if response to frust_events is Yes. Specify frustrating events (check all that apply) - Parents
